MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or Adjudication.

No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel, or Interested Person. The court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: Continued on the court's own motion to June 22, 2026.

Judge: Christine Van Aken. Clerk: Robert Woods. Not Reported. (301/CVA)

Notice Of Motion To Compel Further Responses By Defendant To Form Interrogatories - Employment Law, Set One [Motion 2 Of 4]
Off Calendar Dec-05-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF ARWELIE CABALLERO'S Motion To Compel Further Responses By Defendant To Form Interrogatories - Employment Law, Set One.

The court grants the motion in part and denies it in part as stated below. Meet and confer efforts were adequate. The court orders the City to pay $250 in sanctions to Plaintiff within 30 days. The City's initial discovery responses were insufficient and it was slow to come into compliance with its obligations. But Plaintiff has achieved only limited success with this motion, his moving papers overreached, and his separate statement was unreasonably prolix in laying out concerns about responses the City had already amended.

(see word minutes for complete ruling)

Proposed order submitted.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (301/CVA)

Notice Of Motion To Compel Further Responses By Defendant To Request For Admissions, Set One [Motion 3 Of 4]
Off Calendar Dec-05-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF ARWELIE CABALLERO'S Motion To Compel Further Responses By Defendant To Request For Admissions, Set One.

Plaintiff's motion to compel further responses to his requests for admission is denied as moot. Plaintiff agrees in his reply that the City's belated service of verifications moots the need for further relief. (Reply, pp. 7:28-8:1.)

Plaintiff seeks sanctions. The court orders the City to pay $250 in sanctions to Plaintiff within 30 days. A motion for verifications is a matter of a page or two and Plaintiff's lengthy account of the deficiencies of superseded responses was unnecessary. On the other hand, the City's response to the deficiencies in its initial responses and the time it took to obtain a verification were unreasonably protracted.

Proposed order submitted.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (301/CVA)
